Need Help?

Frequently Asked Questions

Get quick answers to common questions.

Can I use Coverr air conditioner videos for commercial client work?

Yes — all videos in this collection are royalty-free and cleared for commercial use. Each clip is individually reviewed to meet copyright and licensing standards, whether it was filmed by a creator or generated with AI.

Does this collection include real footage or AI video?

Both. This is a hybrid library made up of real, human-shot footage related to air conditioner alongside AI-generated videos. Every video is clearly labeled so you always know what you’re using.

Will using these videos slow down my website?

Not if you select our optimized versions. We offer lightweight, web-ready formats designed for background use — keeping quality high while minimizing load times and improving metrics like LCP.

What if I can’t find the exact air conditioner scene I need?

You can create one instantly using Coverr AI Studio. Just describe the scene — like "air conditioner at sunset" — and the Studio will generate a custom video for you in seconds aligned with our licensing standards.

Do I need to give attribution when using Coverr videos?

No attribution is required. All videos in our stock library are royalty-free and can be used without crediting the creator — though it’s always appreciated.

Can I use Coverr videos in YouTube content or paid ads?

Yes. All stock footage from Coverr can be used in monetized YouTube videos, social media promotions, and client ads — as long as you’re not reselling or redistributing the footage itself as a standalone product.

Is there a watermark on free stock video downloads?

No. None of our free videos — whether real or AI-generated — include watermarks. You get clean, ready-to-use footage.

Can I edit or modify Coverr videos?

Yes. You’re free to trim, crop, or remix our videos. Just make sure the final product follows our license and isn’t redistributed as raw stock content.

What’s the difference between royalty-free and premium videos?

Royalty-free videos include commercial rights, while premium content includes exclusive footage, 4K resolution, and extended licensing protections.
